reasoning:
According to the Wikipedia 'FIDE rankings' page fetched for Nov 2025, Wei Yi has an Elo of 2753. The target is an increase of at least 1% from his mid-Nov 2025 rating of 2753 or 2754. A 1% increase demands gaining ~28 Elo points, jumping to roughly 2781. In top-level classical chess, gaining 28 points from the mid-2750s in a few months requires astronomically high performance (e.g., scoring +6 in a super-tournament). 
1. 2025-11-16: FIDE lists update monthly on the 1st of the month, and Wikipedia tracks this official FIDE update. Thus, the FIDE list presented on Nov 16 will be completely unchanged from Nov 9. The true probability is ~0% but constrained to the minimum allowed 0.05.
2. 2025-12-09: Evaluates the December 2025 FIDE list. A 28-point rise would require an unforeseen, historic classical tournament victory in November. The probability reflects the extremely low base rate.
3. 2026-02-07: Evaluates the February 2026 list, which would include events like Tata Steel in January 2026. While technically possible if he replicates a world-class string of wins, an increase of 28 points in ~3 months remains exceedingly unlikely. Assigned a slightly higher 0.08 to respect increasing uncertainty over the horizon.
